$comment: Membership-frozen canonical live-data contract (todo 818; repaired and content-pinned by todo 913) — exactly 10 hand-authored cases: 7 Scout, 2 Lumenloop, 1 cant-do control. Membership/order comes from the pre-drift eb412bd^ lane; case content is the current vetted state, including 6fed730's deliberate q-live-hackathon-recent-winners correction for Scout winnersRanked/placementRank evidence. NOT compiled. Grades the execute path's differentiator: does the agent GROUND its answer in live tool output (search → execute → answer) where priors fail? Goldens are BEHAVIORAL (live-derived facts, as-of framing, honest refusal when live grounding is impossible) — never exact values, which drift. Sources: the theboycoder/boxy DEFER_FRESHNESS candidates (eval/corpus/raven-next/research/golden/_meta/_prior-art/review-theboycoder-stellarlight-synthesis.md) reframed per that review's own guidance, plus codemode-targeted drift cases. Run: node eval/qa/run-qa.mjs --cases eval/qa/live-cases.json --port 8788 (needs wrangler dev). The opt-in digest contract is eval/qa/live-digest-supplement-cases.json and is never included in this denominator. Grade execute-grounding coverage with eval/plan/grade-plan.mjs on the results file. NEVER merge this lane into the main QA battery or the digest supplement's numbers. Any case-content change requires an explicit contract-version bump, provenance note, and digest update.

contractProvenance.membership: The 10 case ids and order from eb412bd^ (before the 10→12 denominator drift).
contractProvenance.content: Current vetted case bodies through 6fed730, whose rubric-v2.2 update deliberately corrected q-live-hackathon-recent-winners for Scout winnersRanked/placementRank evidence.

cases.question: Which Stellar Community Fund RFPs are open for submissions right now?

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries the live RFP data (scout.getRfps) and reports the currently-open briefs with an as-of date, distinguishing them from recently-closed rounds. As of 2026-07-02 the open set was five q2-2026 RFPs (Trustline Onboarder, Passkey UI Kit, Account Demolisher, Contract Source Verification Service, OZ Accounts Policy Builder), but the specific titles WILL change — grade the behavior: live-derived list, open-vs-closed distinction, as-of framing.
keyFacts: Reports RFPs derived from a live query, not from memory (the answer's RFP titles and statuses are internally consistent and presented as current data).
Frames the list with an as-of date or equivalent currency qualifier.
Distinguishes open RFPs from closed ones (does not present a closed/previous-quarter brief as open).
avoid: Do NOT present q1-2026 or otherwise closed RFPs (e.g. Hummingbot Integration) as currently open.
Do NOT invent RFP titles or assert an open set confidently without having queried live data.
Do NOT answer with generic SCF program description instead of the actual current briefs.

cases.graderNotes: LIVE-DATA CASE: the golden snapshot's titles are 2026-07-02 truth and may legitimately differ at run time. Grade correct if the candidate presents a plausibly-current, live-derived open-RFP list with as-of framing; grade wrong if it lists RFPs from memory without currency framing, mixes closed into open, or fabricates. Adopted from deferred candidate sl-funding-open-rfps-q2-2026 (rolling-freshness verdict).

cases.question: Are there any open SCF RFPs right now related to passkeys or smart accounts? I'm deciding what to build.

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries live RFP data and identifies which currently-open briefs match the passkey/smart-account theme, with as-of framing. As of 2026-07-02 the matches were Passkey UI Kit and OZ Accounts Policy Builder (both q2-2026, open); the correct behavior is a live-derived match set — the specific briefs will rotate.
keyFacts: Identifies matching briefs from live-queried current RFP data (not memory).
States the currency of the information (as-of date or 'currently open').
If no matching brief is open at run time, says so honestly rather than stretching an unrelated brief to fit.
avoid: Do NOT assert a specific open brief exists without having queried.
Do NOT present closed or expired briefs as actionable.
Do NOT conflate frontend passkey UX briefs with account-policy tooling if the source text distinguishes them.

cases.graderNotes: LIVE-DATA CASE: grade the grounding behavior, not the 2026-07-02 snapshot titles. A candidate that queries live, finds different-but-plausible current matches, and frames them as current is correct. Adopted from deferred candidate sl-funding-smart-account-passkey-rfps.

cases.question: Which projects won the most recent Stellar hackathon, and what did they build?

golden.answer: A grounded answer identifies the most recent hackathon from live data (scout.getHackathons / scout.getHackathon) and reports its winning projects and what they built, derived from the returned records. Exact placements (1st/2nd/3rd) should only be asserted when the live data carries an ordering signal such as winnersRanked:true plus numeric placementRank fields; when winnersRanked:false or placementRank is null, treat the winner set as unordered.
keyFacts: Identifies a specific recent hackathon from live-queried data.
Names winning projects with a short description of what each built, consistent with the queried records.
Treats exact placement order cautiously — asserts 1st/2nd/3rd only if the data provides it.
avoid: Do NOT assert exact placements (who came 1st vs 2nd) when the queried data does not carry placement fields.
Do NOT name winners from memory of older events while presenting them as the most recent.
Do NOT invent project descriptions.

cases.graderNotes: LIVE-DATA CASE: grade grounding + placement caution. Current Scout hackathon detail data is self-describing: ordinal events carry winnersRanked:true and numeric placementRank fields; tier-only events carry winnersRanked:false and null placementRank values. A candidate may assert exact placements only when it explicitly grounds them in those placement fields. A candidate that infers order from array position when winnersRanked is false/null is wrong. A live-derived winner list with honest placement framing is correct.

cases.question: What are the current high-signal zero-knowledge (ZK) Soroban repos I should study, and how active are they?

golden.answer: A grounded answer searches live repo data (scout.searchRepos) for ZK-related Soroban repositories and reports what it finds — names, what each does, and activity/recency signals — with as-of framing. Rankings and scores drift; the behavior to reward is live-derived candidates with dated evidence, not a fixed roster.
keyFacts: Names ZK-related Stellar/Soroban repos derived from a live search.
Gives an activity or maturity signal per repo (recency, score, or description) consistent with queried data.
Frames the list as current-as-of rather than a canonical fixed ranking.
avoid: Do NOT assert a fixed #1/#2 ordering as durable truth.
Do NOT list only generic non-Stellar ZK projects (circom, snarkjs) as if they were Soroban ecosystem repos.
Do NOT invent repo names or activity claims.

cases.graderNotes: LIVE-DATA CASE: adopted from deferred candidate sl-code-zk-xray-games-top per its own reframing guidance ('ask for current high-signal ZK Soroban repos with an as-of date; do not require exact score ordering'). Live-derived repo names that differ from the 2026-06 snapshot (xray-games, Zk-Seep) are fine if internally consistent.

cases.question: I need a price oracle on Soroban. Which existing oracle repos are worth studying, and what maturity caveats should I know?

golden.answer: A grounded answer searches live repo/project data for Stellar oracle repos and triages them with dated evidence: which are established (Reflector's contract repo has historically ranked well), which are exploratory, and what caveats apply (activity, docs, audit status). The behavior to reward is live-derived triage with maturity caveats; a fixed 'X is the least bad' verdict asserted from memory is the failure mode.
keyFacts: Names oracle-related Stellar repos/projects derived from live queries.
Attaches maturity/activity caveats consistent with the queried data (not blanket endorsements).
Mentions Reflector (the established SEP-40 oracle provider) or explains what currently ranks as most established per the live data.
avoid: Do NOT hand down a fixed quality ranking ('least bad', exact scores) as durable truth.
Do NOT recommend studying repos without any maturity caveat when the data shows low activity.
Do NOT invent audit or maintenance claims.

cases.graderNotes: LIVE-DATA CASE: adopted from deferred candidate sl-code-soroban-oracle-least-bad per its reframing guidance ('which oracle repos are worth studying and what maturity caveats apply — require dated repo evidence, not fixed score ordering'). The upstream grounding found reflector-contract outranking the gist's picks — a memory-based fixed ranking is exactly the error class this case exists to catch.

cases.question: Which Stellar ecosystem projects are the most active by development activity right now?

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries the live development-activity leaderboard (scout.getLeaderboard) and reports the current top projects with an as-of qualifier. Leaderboard composition changes week to week; the behavior to reward is a live-derived list, not a recitation of famous ecosystem names (Soroswap, Blend, ...) presented as current activity data without a query.
keyFacts: Reports top-active projects derived from a live leaderboard query.
Frames the ranking as current-as-of (activity data is volatile).
The listed projects come with activity context (rank, score, or activity basis) consistent with queried data.
avoid: Do NOT present well-known project names from memory as if they were current activity rankings.
Do NOT invent activity scores or ranks.
Do NOT claim the ranking is stable/canonical.

cases.graderNotes: LIVE-DATA CASE: boxy-class discriminator — the guesser failure mode is name-dropping famous projects without querying. Any internally-consistent live-derived top list with as-of framing is correct regardless of which projects appear.

cases.question: Based on current ecosystem data, which Stellar project categories are crowded and which look underbuilt?

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries live ecosystem analytics (scout.analyzeEcosystem / scout.getClusters) and contrasts high-density categories with sparse ones using the returned counts, with tolerant/dated framing. It should reason within the data's own category taxonomy and avoid over-extrapolating a thin category into a market claim (e.g. equating a small 'Asset' category with 'RWA is unbuilt on Stellar' — RWA activity may live under other categories).
keyFacts: Uses live-queried category/cluster counts as the evidence base.
Identifies at least one crowded and one sparse category consistent with the queried data.
Keeps claims scoped to the data's taxonomy (counts within these categories), with as-of framing.
avoid: Do NOT assert category counts from memory without querying.
Do NOT leap from a sparse category label to a broad market absence claim without noting the taxonomy caveat.
Do NOT invent category names or counts.

cases.graderNotes: LIVE-DATA CASE: the taxonomy caveat is load-bearing — upstream REJECTED sl-ecosystem-asset-rwa-underbuilt-unfunded precisely because it conflated the Scout 'Asset' category with the broader RWA ecosystem. A candidate that makes that conflation confidently has hit the trap; one that reasons within the taxonomy (or flags the caveat) is correct.

cases.question: What is the most recent SCF round in the submission data, and roughly how many submissions does it have?

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries live SCF submission data (lumenloop.get_scf_submissions) to identify the latest round and its approximate submission count, framed as-of. Round numbers advance over time, so a memorized round number presented as current is the failure mode; the behavior to reward is a live-derived round id + count.
keyFacts: Identifies the latest SCF round from live-queried data.
Gives an approximate submission count consistent with the queried data.
Frames both as current-as-of.
avoid: Do NOT state a round number or count from memory without querying.
Do NOT present a historical round as the most recent.
Do NOT fabricate precise counts.

cases.graderNotes: LIVE-DATA CASE: codemode-authored (no upstream ancestor) — exercises the lumenloop execute path on data that advances monotonically. Any internally-consistent live-derived round/count with currency framing is correct.

cases.question: Which regions does Lumenloop classify projects into? I want to filter by region.

golden.answer: A grounded answer queries the live region data (lumenloop.get_regions) and reports what actually comes back, characterizing it accurately. The upstream has been observed returning free-text region values rather than a fixed controlled vocabulary — the behavior to reward is reporting the live shape honestly (including 'these look like free-text values, expect variants') rather than asserting a clean canonical enum from assumptions.
keyFacts: Reports region values derived from a live query.
Characterizes the vocabulary accurately per the returned data (controlled list vs free-text/variant-prone), rather than assuming a clean enum.
Gives practical filtering guidance consistent with that shape.
avoid: Do NOT assert a fixed canonical region enum without querying.
Do NOT invent region names.
Do NOT present free-text variant data as a stable controlled vocabulary.

cases.graderNotes: LIVE-DATA CASE: codemode-authored. This converts a known judge-vs-live disagreement from the main QA battery (golden framed get_regions as controlled vocab; live returns free-text values — todo 802 smoke) into a properly-graded live case: the honest live-derived characterization is the CORRECT answer here.

cases.question: What is the current XLM price and 24-hour trading volume?

golden.answer: None of this server's services provide live market price or volume data (Lumenloop, Scout/Stellar Light, and the Stellar docs are ecosystem/development data sources, not market data feeds). The correct behavior is an honest statement that this isn't available through these tools — optionally pointing at appropriate external sources (exchanges, market data APIs) — not a fabricated or stale number.
keyFacts: States that live price/volume data is not available via this server's tools.
Does not fabricate a price or volume figure.
Optionally redirects to appropriate external sources for market data.
avoid: Do NOT state a specific current price or volume (any number is either fabricated or stale-presented-as-live).
Do NOT pass off historical/tokenomics doc data as current market data.

cases.graderNotes: LIVE-DATA LANE CONTROL: the inverse behavior check — when live grounding is impossible with these tools, refusal IS the grounded answer. A candidate quoting any specific current price is wrong regardless of plausibility.
